relatedness_reasoning: Inbound file provides eligible customer list with product details; outbound file contains marketing offer scripts targeting those customers. Marketing department receives eligible customers and produces offer scripts for Customer Services, forming a logical pipeline step. Both files reference product eligibility, customer segments, and campaign/offer metadata.

mappings.reasoning: No customer or offer identifier exists in inbound; likely system-generated by Marketing.
Offer script name likely derives from or references the investment product name from inbound metadata.
Inbound target_department is Marketing, which matches the outbound department_from enum value.
Customer Services is the receiving department; not present in inbound schema.
Offer scripts created after eligible customer list generated; likely derived from or based on inbound generation timestamp.
Effective date may be set relative to or after the eligible customer list generation date.
No lifecycle status field exists in inbound; determined by Marketing workflow.
Target segment combines product name and customer allocation tier to define the cohort for the offer.
Email presence in inbound suggests email_template as a plausible script_type, though other channels may also be used.
No duration or timing data exists in inbound; determined by script content and delivery channel.
Script content synthesizes product details, eligibility rules, and allocation recommendations from inbound.
No call-to-action template exists in inbound; created by Marketing based on campaign strategy.
No success metric or KPI definition exists in inbound; determined by Marketing campaign objectives.
Total scripts may correspond to or be derived from the count of eligible customers.
Last updated timestamp likely reflects when offer scripts were generated based on inbound eligibility list.
No version identifier exists in inbound; managed independently by outbound schema.
